trivia_bullets.text: Creature type: Kor Cleric (not Kor Ally — no Ally subtype, no Rally). The Stone Havens are canonical Kor settlement-network infrastructure on Zendikar, referenced in the flavor text of Cliffside Lookout (BFZ-20): a distributed system of kor-maintained outposts that serve as refuge and resupply points. A Kor Cleric stationed at a Stone Haven is a field medic in a wartime refugee network, not a front-line combatant — consistent with the oracle text ({W},{T}: You gain 1 life) representing sustained low-intensity healing. [Scryfall: BFZ-51 type_line + oracle_text; Cliffside Lookout BFZ-20 flavor text cross-reference, per wave 41 ip_cleared note]
sources: Cliffside Lookout BFZ-20 flavor text, per ip_cleared_reason in card frontmatter

trivia_bullets.text: Flavor text: "These days, soldiers never stick around long enough for a proper healing. They just want me to knit them up so they can hurry back to the fight." No speaker is attributed. The voice is exhausted-caretaker, not heroic — the medic is frustrated by the tempo of the war, the way injured fighters refuse complete rest. The phrase 'knit them up' is a period-appropriate idiom for wound closure. [Scryfall: BFZ-51 flavor_text]

Stats: 1/3 for {1}{W} with a {W},{T} activated lifegain ability. The defensive stat-line (high toughness, low power) fits the medic archetype. In BFZ draft, it was a late pick: functional but slow, outclassed by more aggressive white two-drops. EDHREC rank 25,264 — occasionally seen in dedicated lifegain Commander lists as a cheap, repeatable trigger source for cards like Archangel of Thune. Pauper-legal. [Scryfall: oracle_text + legalities + edhrec_rank]
